response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates strong individual-contributor engineering skills across backend systems and microservices at scale, the role requires executive leadership—specifically proven ability to scale teams from 10–30+ people and architect production multi-agent AI systems. The resume shows no team management experience and only one month in an AI-infrastructure role, neither of which satisfies the core CTO requirements. The lack of fintech domain knowledge and evidence of operating AI systems with financial consequences further weakens the fit.
strengths: 10+ years of engineering with deep backend/microservices expertise handling millions of requests at scale (Dunnhumby)
Hands-on experience building event-driven platforms and full-stack technical depth across multiple languages and frameworks
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP, agentic workflows) demonstrates adaptability and awareness of modern AI tooling
concerns: No demonstrated team leadership or management experience; critical hard requirement to scale engineering from 10–30+ people
Only one month in current AI infrastructure role with no evidence of designing or operating production multi-agent systems in career history
Minimal fintech domain knowledge and no evidence of operating systems in regulated or high-stakes financial environments
